### Post by denday on 2006-08-28
Help.....

Everytime I restart my Ubuntu Server. Its DNS setting back to an empty file. 

Before Restart :
```

# Dynamic resolv.conf(5) file for glibc resolver(3) generated by resolvconf(8)
#     DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE BY HAND -- YOUR CHANGES WILL BE OVERWRITTEN
nameserver 167.205.22.123
~
~
~

```

After Restart
```

# Dynamic resolv.conf(5) file for glibc resolver(3) generated by resolvconf(8)
#     DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE BY HAND -- YOUR CHANGES WILL BE OVERWRITTEN
~
~
~
~

```

Anyone know how to fix it. [-o< 
thanks.

---

### Post by tribaal on 2006-08-28
You need to make the change in you /etc/resolv.conf fore for the changes to be permanent.

;)

- trib'

---

### Post by denday on 2006-08-28
I have create that in /etc/resolv.conf, more over I set a permission to 000 so anyone could't change it, but unlucky when I reboot my setting overwritten again by server so my DNS setting empty again.

### Post by cracnup on 2006-08-30
the first (and probably obvious question) is: does this server had a static or dynamic address?  I'm not yet an ubuntu user (downloading an ISO to try it out now), but I've never seen a linux install that tried to overwrite /etc/resolv.conf with static IP addressing...

I see that you tried changing permissions on the file... have you tried setting the immutable flag on the file?  That should lock it down so that nothing can change it.  

[http://linuxhelp.blogspot.com/2005/11/make-your-files-immutable-which-even.html](http://linuxhelp.blogspot.com/2005/11/make-your-files-immutable-which-even.html)    for more info.
